prompt: A deep-sea diver in complete darkness descending through pitch-black water using only a rope or cable as tactile guidance. The diver's hands grip the rope, feeling its texture to navigate downward. Water is black, only the diver's headlamp illuminates immediate surroundings—no global view of the ocean floor below. The diver's posture is careful, methodical, focusing only on the next meter of rope. Pressure suit, heavy equipment. The diver feels water pressure and current direction changing subtly with each descent. Cinematic underwater lighting, moody blue-black tones, photographic realism. The sense of moving downward through unknown depths by sensing only the immediate path. No diagrams, no labels, no text, no annotations, no compass, no maps. Shot from a side angle showing both the diver and the rope extending into darkness above and below. Negative prompt: No diagrams, no HUD overlays, no sonar visualizations, no floating text, no labels, no maps, no schematic overlays, no abstract graphics, no glowing effects, no equations, no UI elements. Keep it photorealistic and grounded underwater photography.
metaphor: A diver descends through darkness using only a rope, feeling the way. Each movement follows the rope's direction.
